title: Hands-on Exercise 3
text: This hands-on exercise recreates the key interactive visualisation techniques from Chapter 3 of R for Visual Analytics. The focus is on using ggiraph, plotly, DT, and crosstalk to make static R graphics interactive.

title: Hands-on Exercise 3
text: This hands-on exercise recreates the key interactive visualisation techniques from Chapter 3 of R for Visual Analytics. The focus is on using ggiraph, plotly, DT, and crosstalk to make static R graphics interactive.

title: Hands-on Exercise 3
text:
Setup


Code
if (!require(pacman)) install.packages("pacman")

pacman::p_load(
  ggiraph,
  plotly,
  patchwork,
  DT,
  tidyverse,
  crosstalk
)

exam_data <- read_csv("Exam_data.csv")

title: Hands-on Exercise 3
Hands-on Exercise 3
text:
1.1 Tooltip Showing Student ID


Code
p <- ggplot(exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_dotplot_interactive(
    aes(tooltip = ID),
    stackgroups = TRUE,
    binwidth = 1,
    method = "histodot"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Maths Scores",
    x = "Maths Score"
  )

girafe(
  ggobj = p,
  width_svg = 7,
  height_svg = 4
)

title: Hands-on Exercise 3
text:
1.2 Tooltip Showing Multiple Information


Code
exam_data <- exam_data %>%
  mutate(
    tooltip_text = paste0(
      "ID: ", ID,
      "\nClass: ", CLASS,
      "\nGender: ", GENDER,
      "\nRace: ", RACE,
      "\nMaths: ", MATHS
    )
  )

p <- ggplot(exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_dotplot_interactive(
    aes(tooltip = tooltip_text),
    stackgroups = TRUE,
    binwidth = 1,
    method = "histodot"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL) +
  labs(
    title = "Maths Score Distribution with Detailed Tooltip",
    x = "Maths Score"
  )

girafe(
  ggobj = p,
  width_svg = 8,
  height_svg = 4
)

title: Hands-on Exercise 3
Hands-on Exercise 3
text:
1.4 Hover Effect by Class


Code
p <- ggplot(exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_dotplot_interactive(
    aes(data_id = CLASS),
    stackgroups = TRUE,
    binwidth = 1,
    method = "histodot"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL) +
  labs(
    title = "Hover Highlight by Class",
    x = "Maths Score"
  )

girafe(
  ggobj = p,
  width_svg = 8,
  height_svg = 4
)

title: Hands-on Exercise 3
text:
1.5 Styled Hover Effect


Code
p <- ggplot(exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_dotplot_interactive(
    aes(data_id = CLASS),
    stackgroups = TRUE,
    binwidth = 1,
    method = "histodot"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL) +
  labs(
    title = "Styled Hover Highlight by Class",
    x = "Maths Score"
  )

girafe(
  ggobj = p,
  width_svg = 8,
  height_svg = 4,
  options = list(
    opts_hover(css = "fill: #202020;"),
    opts_hover_inv(css = "opacity:0.2;")
  )
)

title: Hands-on Exercise 3
text:
1.6 Tooltip and Hover Combined


Code
p <- ggplot(exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_dotplot_interactive(
    aes(
      tooltip = tooltip_text,
      data_id = CLASS
    ),
    stackgroups = TRUE,
    binwidth = 1,
    method = "histodot"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL) +
  labs(
    title = "Tooltip and Hover Combined",
    x = "Maths Score"
  )

girafe(
  ggobj = p,
  width_svg = 8,
  height_svg = 4,
  options = list(
    opts_tooltip(css = tooltip_css),
    opts_hover(css = "fill: #202020;"),
    opts_hover_inv(css = "opacity:0.2;")
  )
)

title: Hands-on Exercise 3
text:
1.7 Coordinated Multiple Views with ggiraph


Code
p1 <- ggplot(exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_dotplot_interactive(
    aes(data_id = ID, tooltip = tooltip_text),
    stackgroups = TRUE,
    binwidth = 1,
    method = "histodot"
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 100)) +
  scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL) +
  labs(
    title = "Maths",
    x = "Maths Score"
  )

p2 <- ggplot(exam_data, aes(x = ENGLISH)) +
  geom_dotplot_interactive(
    aes(data_id = ID, tooltip = tooltip_text),
    stackgroups = TRUE,
    binwidth = 1,
    method = "histodot"
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 100)) +
  scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL) +
  labs(
    title = "English",
    x = "English Score"
  )

girafe(
  code = print(p1 + p2),
  width_svg = 9,
  height_svg = 4,
  options = list(
    opts_tooltip(css = tooltip_css),
    opts_hover(css = "fill: #202020;"),
    opts_hover_inv(css = "opacity:0.2;")
  )
)

title: Hands-on Exercise 3
text:
2.1 Basic Interactive Scatter Plot


Code
plot_ly(
  data = exam_data,
  x = ~MATHS,
  y = ~ENGLISH,
  type = "scatter",
  mode = "markers"
)

title: Hands-on Exercise 3
text:
2.2 Scatter Plot Coloured by Race


Code
plot_ly(
  data = exam_data,
  x = ~ENGLISH,
  y = ~MATHS,
  color = ~RACE,
  type = "scatter",
  mode = "markers",
  text = ~paste(
    "ID:", ID,
    "<br>Class:", CLASS,
    "<br>Gender:", GENDER,
    "<br>Race:", RACE,
    "<br>English:", ENGLISH,
    "<br>Maths:", MATHS
  ),
  hoverinfo = "text"
)

title: Hands-on Exercise 3
text:
2.3 ggplot Converted to Interactive Plotly


Code
p <- ggplot(exam_data, aes(x = MATHS, y = ENGLISH)) +
  geom_point(size = 1) +
  coord_cartesian(
    xlim = c(0, 100),
    ylim = c(0, 100)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "English vs Maths",
    x = "Maths Score",
    y = "English Score"
  )

ggplotly(p)

title: Hands-on Exercise 3
text:
2.4 Coordinated Multiple Views with plotly


Code
d <- highlight_key(exam_data)

p1 <- ggplot(d, aes(x = MATHS, y = ENGLISH)) +
  geom_point(size = 1) +
  coord_cartesian(
    xlim = c(0, 100),
    ylim = c(0, 100)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "English vs Maths",
    x = "Maths",
    y = "English"
  )

p2 <- ggplot(d, aes(x = MATHS, y = SCIENCE)) +
  geom_point(size = 1) +
  coord_cartesian(
    xlim = c(0, 100),
    ylim = c(0, 100)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Science vs Maths",
    x = "Maths",
    y = "Science"
  )

subplot(
  ggplotly(p1),
  ggplotly(p2),
  titleX = TRUE,
  titleY = TRUE
)
